_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-42R Racepinephrine (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-42R: Racepinephrine — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
pharmacological_category: Adrenergic Agonist — Topical Airway Vasoconstrictor / Bronchodilator
indications: Pediatric stridor at rest WITHOUT obstruction (croup — laryngotracheobronchitis)
Upper airway mucosal edema with stridor (pediatric)
contraindications: Known mechanical airway obstruction (foreign body, epiglottitis — do NOT nebulize)
Adult use — PEDIATRIC ONLY in Michigan EMS protocol
Known hypersensitivity to racepinephrine or epinephrine
precautions: PEDIATRIC USE ONLY — not indicated for adults in Michigan EMS protocol
Only for stridor at rest without obstruction — if obstruction suspected (foreign body, epiglottitis), do NOT use; manage airway directly
Rebound effect: airway edema may recur as medication wears off (1-2 hours) — all patients receiving racepinephrine require transport and hospital evaluation
Monitor heart rate — racepinephrine can cause tachycardia
expected_effects: Vasoconstriction of upper airway mucosa → reduction of subglottic edema
side_effects: Rebound airway edema after effect wears off
notes: Standard dose: 0.5 mL of 2.25% racepinephrine solution in 3 mL NS nebulized
Croup/stridor at rest only — must not have obstruction
Transport required regardless of improvement — rebound edema risk
Nebulize via mask held near face if child cannot tolerate mask on face ('blow-by')

dosing.population: Pediatric (≤14 yr) — croup / stridor at rest
dosing.dose: 0.5 mL of 2.25% racepinephrine solution in 3 mL NS via nebulizer
dosing.notes: PEDIATRIC ONLY; stridor at rest WITHOUT obstruction; transport required regardless of improvement due to rebound risk
